content:
✅ Why This Works:
• Adds agency to the game without compromising academic rigor.
• Encourages students to use XP strategically instead of hoarding it.
• Balances flexibility with structured constraints.

📜 2. Sacrificial XP Trades – High-Risk, High-Reward Player Interactions

📜 Students can now “trade” XP for the benefit of others—but with a built-in penalty.

✔ XP Trades follow the “Blessing & Curse” system:
• The giver receives a minor buff (e.g., +5 XP next challenge, one-time exemption from a minor penalty).
• The receiver gets the full XP amount transferred but suffers a minor debuff (e.g., a more difficult challenge next round).
• Some XP trades require a third party to mediate—turning it into a class event.

Example: “The Pact of the Scholar” (XP Trade Scenario)
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 262 (chunk 262/555)
